findings.content: Co-located tests under __tests__/: .test.tsx (Vitest) + .ct.tsx (Playwright CT). Tests must cover the acceptance criteria including edge/error paths.
Reuse an existing utility/component before writing anything generic. The fix must reuse an existing CT convention, not invent new test machinery.
Every change impacts as little code as possible; never 'fix' unrelated code. Confines the fix to the failing specs.
Dropdown wraps Radix DropdownMenu; click-outside dismissal is Radix-owned. The fix stays at the test layer, not Radix internals.
Type-check: npm run typecheck:node && npm run typecheck:web — verification command for a hygiene AC.
Lint: eslint --cache . — verification command for a hygiene AC; must pass on changed files before task completion.
Verification runs at task boundaries (no per-edit hooks); CT/unit suites are the mechanical gate.

Radix overlay click-outside CT tests must wait for overlay readiness before page.mouse.click; DismissableLayer arms its pointerdown listener via setTimeout(0), so an immediate outside click races the arm. Avoid bare waitForTimeout magic numbers; open-at-mount (Modal CT) or await a real readiness signal.
Playwright CT tests must reproduce the full context (tokens.css import, production className scope) — keep fixtures faithful when editing CT specs.
Memory confirms bug 003 is test-only / not a production defect — fix scope is the spec layer, production Dropdown behavior unchanged.
CT command: playwright test -c playwright.config.ts (@playwright/experimental-ct-react), real-browser focus/keyboard fidelity — the verification command for the CT ACs.
Radix unified package owns focus-trap, keyboard nav, and positioning (incl. dismissal) for Dropdown/Modal/Toast — dismissal is Radix-owned; the fix is test-layer.
Dropdown wraps Radix DropdownMenu, Modal wraps Radix Dialog; Modal CT is the sibling overlay test on the same substrate (canonical readiness pattern to reuse).
Dropdown.ct.tsx is the failing CT; Modal.ct.tsx and nested-overlays.ct.tsx are sibling overlay CTs that must not regress.
Dropdown.tsx = controlled dropdown over Radix DropdownMenu; Dropdown.stories.tsx holds the CT fixtures (DropdownFixture) — the fix touches test/fixture files only, no production component.
Tests split: .test.tsx (Vitest, jsdom) vs .ct.tsx (Playwright CT, Chromium) — the click-outside behavior is CT-only (needs a real browser).

Setup-timing race: page.mouse.click(corner) fires before Radix DismissableLayer arms its document pointerdown listener (attached via setTimeout(0), index.mjs:265, during the 140ms entry animation). Proven: SETTLE=0 -> 0/5 dismiss, SETTLE=100ms -> 5/5.
Two CT tests fail: Dropdown.ct.tsx:185 (AC-4 dismiss on outside click) and :236 (AC-3 focus-return after click-outside, downstream). CT suite 125/127, these the sole failures.

Test-only fix: in both specs, after the menu is visible, await a concrete overlay-readiness signal (entry-animation completion) before the corner click; mirrors Modal CT open-at-mount (Modal.ct.tsx:127). Zero shipped code; both strict not.toBeVisible assertions kept verbatim.
NOT a production defect: Modal CT passes the identical page.mouse.click outside dispatch (armed early); a settle delay makes Dropdown deterministic. Production click-outside works for real users.
Keep expect(menu).not.toBeVisible() strict — remove the race, do not weaken/loosen the AC-4/AC-3 assertions. No production behavior change.
Bare waitForTimeout(~150ms) works (matches experiment) but is a brittle magic-number that rots under slower CI — rejected in favor of a real readiness signal.

decision_points.description: Which CT specs does this fix cover — only the two failing Dropdown specs, or also proactively harden the sibling overlay CTs that share the same arm-race risk (Modal.ct.tsx, nested-overlays.ct.tsx)?
valid_implementations: only the two failing Dropdown specs
Dropdown specs + proactively harden sibling overlay CTs (Modal, nested-overlays)
decision_points.user_answer: Only the two failing Dropdown specs (Dropdown.ct.tsx:185 + :236). Sibling overlay CTs (Modal, nested-overlays) currently pass and stay untouched — smallest blast radius.

decision_points.description: Readiness-signal mechanism for the pre-corner-click wait (the research recommends awaiting entry-animation completion; a bare fixed timeout is the rejected runner-up).
valid_implementations: await a concrete overlay-readiness signal (entry-animation completion) — recommended
leave exact mechanism to /plan; spec asserts deterministic outcome only
decision_points.user_answer: Spec mandates awaiting a concrete overlay-readiness signal (entry-animation completion) before the corner click — deterministic, no bare magic-number timeout.

decision_points.description: no relevant decision point for existing_behavior
no relevant decision point for data_flow_state
no relevant decision point for edge_cases
no relevant decision point for ui_ux_details
no relevant decision point for breaking_changes

mandatory_reads.n_a_reason: A Playwright CT spec is a test entry point — no project module imports it, so there are no callers to read.

overview: Two Playwright component tests for the Dropdown primitive's click-outside dismissal are flaky-to-failing because each fires its outside click before Radix's DismissableLayer has armed its outside-click detection. This spec makes both tests deterministic by gating the outside click on a concrete overlay-readiness signal — a test-only change that touches no production code and keeps the existing strict assertions verbatim.
current_state: Dropdown (src/renderer/src/components/molecules/Dropdown.tsx:204) wraps Radix DropdownMenu (modal by default); click-outside dismissal is owned by Radix's DismissableLayer. Two CT tests exercise it: 'AC-4 — clicking outside the menu closes it' (src/renderer/src/components/molecules/__tests__/Dropdown.ct.tsx:185) and 'AC-3 — focus return after click-outside' (Dropdown.ct.tsx:236). Both open the menu via trigger.click(), assert the menu visible, then immediately fire page.mouse.click at a viewport coordinate (Dropdown.ct.tsx:198 and :253) and assert expect(menu).not.toBeVisible(). They fail: research (research/2026-06-27-003-dropdown-ct-click.md) proved a setup-timing race — Radix DismissableLayer attaches its document pointerdown listener via setTimeout(0) (node_modules/@radix-ui/react-dismissable-layer/dist/index.mjs:265) while the 140ms entry animation runs, so a click in the same tick the menu becomes visible is never classified as outside and onDismiss never fires (measured: zero of five runs dismissed with no settle, all five with a 100ms settle). The sibling Modal CT (src/renderer/src/components/molecules/__tests__/Modal.ct.tsx:127) passes the identical page.mouse.click outside dispatch because it mounts the overlay open, arming the layer long before its click — confirming this is a test-timing defect, not a production defect. Per docs/architecture.md the CT suite runs via 'playwright test -c playwright.config.ts'; these two are the sole failures (125 of 127 passing). The CT spec has been unchanged since it was added in 001-ui-primitives, so it has been racy since inception.
desired_behavior: Both CT tests pass deterministically. Each test, after asserting the menu is visible and before firing the outside corner click, waits for a concrete overlay-readiness signal (the menu's entry animation having completed) so Radix's DismissableLayer is guaranteed armed. The existing strict assertions are preserved exactly — expect(menu).not.toBeVisible() for dismissal (Dropdown.ct.tsx:200) and the dismissal + expect(trigger).toBeFocused() for focus-return (Dropdown.ct.tsx:256, :259). No production code changes: only the two test cases (and, if needed, their fixture in Dropdown.stories.tsx) are edited. No bare fixed-delay timeout is introduced. The rest of the CT suite stays green.

affected_areas.impact: Edit the two failing test cases (the outside clicks at :198 and :253): insert an overlay-readiness wait before each corner click. Strict assertions unchanged.
Only if the readiness wait needs a fixture affordance (e.g. a stable test hook). Preferred approach needs no fixture change; listed so /plan can confirm.

acceptance_criteria.statement: WHEN a user clicks outside an open Dropdown menu, the Dropdown shall close the menu.
The Dropdown component-test suite shall keep all of its currently-passing tests passing.
WHEN the CT test 'clicking outside the menu closes it' (Dropdown.ct.tsx:185) runs, the Dropdown menu shall become not visible so the dismissal assertion passes.
acceptance_criteria.verification_command: npx playwright test -c playwright.config.ts --grep 'clicking outside the menu closes it'

acceptance_criteria.statement: WHEN the CT test 'focus return after click-outside' (Dropdown.ct.tsx:236) runs, the Dropdown menu shall close and focus shall return to the trigger button.
The two click-outside Dropdown component tests shall pass deterministically across repeated runs.
The changed test files shall pass type-check and lint.
WHILE applying the fix, the system shall keep the existing strict dismissal and focus-return assertions unchanged and unweakened.
The changed test files shall contain no leftover debug artifacts such as console statements, debugger statements, or focused or skipped tests.

ac_subsection_na.tooling_artifact_presence: No tooling artifact is added or removed; this is a behavior fix to two existing test cases.
ac_subsection_na.ci_pipeline: No CI/pipeline config change; deterministic passing is asserted by the CT acceptance criteria in §5.3.
ac_subsection_na.documentation: No docs/ change required; the bugs/003 record lifecycle is handled by /verify, not this spec.

out_of_scope.content: Modifying Radix or the production Dropdown component (src/renderer/src/components/molecules/Dropdown.tsx) — click-outside dismissal is Radix-owned and works correctly in production.
Introducing a bare fixed-delay waitForTimeout as the fix mechanism (brittle magic-number; rejected by the research).
Hardening the sibling overlay CT specs (Modal.ct.tsx, nested-overlays.ct.tsx) that share the same arm-race risk — they pass today and are excluded per the scope decision.

constraints.content: Search the codebase for an existing utility/helper/component before writing anything generic or reusable — reuse the Modal CT overlay-readiness pattern (Modal.ct.tsx:127) rather than inventing new test machinery.

Co-located tests under __tests__/ next to the code, split .test.tsx (Vitest) and .ct.tsx (Playwright CT); the click-outside behavior is CT-only and the fix stays in the .ct.tsx spec (and fixture, if needed).
Production Dropdown click-outside dismissal behavior must not change — it works for real users; only the test's timing is corrected.

open_questions.content: Exact readiness API for the pre-click wait (e.g. awaiting element.getAnimations() completion vs a waitForFunction on the menu's computed opacity) — deferred to /plan; spec mandates a concrete readiness signal, not a fixed delay.

risks.risk: Under prefers-reduced-motion (or any zero-animation config), the entry animation is disabled, so an animation-completion readiness signal could resolve immediately and let the arm-race resurface.
risks.mitigation: Readiness signal must not rely solely on animation presence; /plan picks a signal that holds when animations are off (e.g. a microtask/rAF settle or polling the armed state), or the test enables motion.

risks.risk: The readiness wait could mask a genuine future production dismissal regression (test waits, then still asserts).
risks.mitigation: Assertions stay strict (not.toBeVisible / toBeFocused), so a real dismissal break still fails the test after the wait.

approval_summary:

- **What changes**: Two Playwright component tests for the Dropdown primitive's click-outside dismissal are flaky-to-failing because each fires its outside click before Radix's DismissableLayer has armed its outside-click detection. This spec makes both tes...
- **Files affected**: 3 files across 3 areas
- **Acceptance criteria**: 8 testable criteria across 3 AC categories
- **Out of scope**: Modifying Radix or the production Dropdown component (src/renderer/src/component; Introducing a bare fixed-delay waitForTimeout as the fix mechanism (brittle magi; Hardening the sibling overlay CT specs (Modal.ct.tsx, nested-overlays.ct.tsx) th

Please review and either approve or request changes. Once approved, run `/plan` to create the technical implementation plan.
plan_handoff_block:
## Manual next step — run /plan

A structured handoff (specs/008-dropdown-ct-dismiss/handoff.json) is written for /plan. /plan auto-discovers it on its first run and reads the upstream plan-seeds — but you still launch /plan manually (there is no auto-dispatch from /specify). Restart Claude Code (exit and relaunch the CLI/app so the newly installed command is picked up), then run the command below in this repo. The spec path is explicit so /plan does not need most-recent-spec discovery:

~~~
/plan specs/008-dropdown-ct-dismiss/spec.md
~~~

Minimum handoff data:
- Spec status: Draft
- Spec type: bug_fix
- AC count: 8 across 3 subsections (5.1: 0, 5.2: 3, 5.3: 3, 5.4: 0, 5.5: 0, 5.6: 0, 5.7: 2)
- Decision-point coverage: 2 answered, 0 default-applied, 0 deferred-OOS, 0 deferred-open-question
- Affected areas: 3 across node_modules, src
- Out-of-scope items: 3
- Open questions: 1
- Constraints: 6
- Risks: 2
- Phase 1.5 finding coverage: 100% (all findings landed)

Reference: specs/008-dropdown-ct-dismiss/spec.md

open_question_resolutions.resolution_text: Readiness API = await menu.evaluate(getAnimations().finished) + one in-page setTimeout(0) macrotask-boundary yield; deterministic and motion-independent, not a fixed-delay timeout.
